idea: Unemployment Initial Claims Spike Weekly Reversal

data.source: FRED series ICSA (Initial Claims weekly) via fred_series adapter

behavior: Weekly initial jobless claims spike above 4-week moving average by >15%, then revert within 2 weeks as employers pause hiring freezes temporarily.

market_impact.rationale: Consumer discretionary outperforms when labor volatility subsides and hiring uncertainty clears.

trade_logic.entry: If ICSA > (4-week MA × 1.15) and prior week was normal, enter long XLY
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 7 trading days or when ICSA falls back below 1.05× 4-week MA

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Initial claims data is published weekly and spikes/reversions occur multiple times per quarter in response to seasonal adjustments and policy changes.

idea: Commercial Real Estate Distress Floor Collapse Signal

data.source: FRED series MORTGAGE30US (30-year mortgage rate) via fred_series adapter

behavior: 30-year mortgage rates drop >0.5% in a single week, signaling flight-to-safety demand, which precedes weakness in REITs and property valuations.

market_impact.rationale: Sharp mortgage rate drops indicate investor panic and reduced appetite for real estate risk assets.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly mortgage rate drop > 50 bps from prior week close, short XLRE
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if rates stabilize/rise for 2 consecutive weeks

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Mortgage rates publish weekly and volatility spikes occur monthly on average during Fed policy shifts and macro uncertainty.

idea: Natural Gas Demand Surge Winter Heating Panic

data.source: EIA natural gas weekly storage and demand via eia_electricity adapter (extended to gas data)

behavior: EIA reports weekly natural gas storage drawdowns >50 Bcf above seasonal norms during cold snaps, driving energy utility demand spikes.

market_impact.rationale: Utilities benefit from high demand periods and pass through energy cost recoveries.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly gas storage draw > 50 Bcf above 5-year average for that week, long XLU
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if storage normalization resumes

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Winter heating demand triggers multiple storage spikes per season; this fires most weeks November–March and occasionally in shoulder months.

idea: Retail Apparel Price Collapse Google Search Decay

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'clearance clothing sales' via google_trends adapter

behavior: Google searches for clearance clothing fall >30% week-over-week after seasonal peaks, signaling buyer exhaustion and inventory overhang clearing.

market_impact.rationale: Apparel weakness in search signals consumer pullback in discretionary spending and potential markdowns ahead.

trade_logic.entry: If 'clearance clothing sales' searches drop >30% from prior week and volume is above median, short XLY
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 9 trading days or if searches rebound above 90-day median

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal clothing clearance cycles drive weekly search swings; collapses occur post-holiday and end-of-season, firing multiple times per quarter.

idea: Truck Tonnage Decline Logistics Contraction Shock

data.source: American Trucking Associations weekly tonnage index via html_table scrape adapter

behavior: Weekly truck tonnage declines >5% from prior week, indicating sharp pullback in freight demand and supply chain contraction.

market_impact.rationale: Trucks move 70% of US freight; sharp tonnage drops precede industrial production weakness.

trade_logic.entry: If ATA tonnage index drops >5% week-over-week, short XLI
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if tonnage stabilizes (change <±2% for 2 weeks)

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly data with high volatility; tonnage declines trigger 2–3 times per quarter in response to demand shocks.

idea: Oil Prices Bounce Off Support Political Supply Fear

data.source: EIA weekly crude inventories and price data via eia_electricity adapter and price_only for crude (CL=F)

behavior: Crude oil prices fall to 2-year support level, then reverse sharply on news of supply disruption (OPEC+ cuts, geopolitical event), driving energy sector rebound.

market_impact.rationale: Energy stocks rally when oil prices stabilize at support after sharp declines.

trade_logic.entry: If crude price closes >5% below 52-week MA and EIA inventory reports decline, long XLE
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if crude breaks support again

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Oil bounces off support 2–4 times per year; geopolitical events trigger supply fear spikes monthly on average.

idea: EV Charger Network Expansion Sprint Demand Surge

data.source: OpenChargeMap daily charger counts by region via openchargemap adapter

behavior: New EV charging stations added at >15% monthly growth rate after policy incentive announcements, driving bullish sentiment in EV and auto stocks.

market_impact.rationale: EV infrastructure expansion signals robust consumer adoption and OEM capex confidence.

trade_logic.entry: If monthly charger count growth > 15% YoY and daily count increases 3 days in a row, long XLY
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 12 trading days or if growth rate falls below 8%

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Infrastructure build-outs are announced quarterly; monthly surges align with federal/state incentive rollouts, which occur multiple times per year.

idea: Temperature Extremes Power Demand Spike Utility Boost

data.source: Open-Meteo daily temperatures (highs/lows) for major US metro areas via weather_series adapter

behavior: Daily high temperatures exceed 95°F or fall below 0°F for 3+ consecutive days in major population centers, spiking HVAC load and electricity demand.

market_impact.rationale: Extreme weather drives peak demand periods and higher utility margins.

trade_logic.entry: If 3+ consecutive days of extreme temps (>95F or <0F) in cities covering >50M people, long XLU
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 7 trading days or when temperatures return to normal range

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Temperature extremes occur throughout the year (summer heat and winter cold waves); multi-day stretches trigger 5–8 times annually.

idea: Seismic Activity Regional Construction Halt Shock

data.source: USGS earthquake activity (magnitude >4.5) via earthquake_activity adapter

behavior: Magnitude 5.0+ earthquake in a major metro area triggers 2–4 week construction permit freeze, reducing materials demand and industrial activity.

market_impact.rationale: Materials demand drops when construction halts for safety assessments and permit reviews.

trade_logic.entry: If magnitude 5.0+ quake occurs in CA, TX, or NY region, short XLB
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 14 trading days or if construction activity resumes (permits reissued)

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Moderate earthquakes occur 1–2 times per month in active regions; magnitude 5.0+ hits major metros 3–4 times per year on average.

idea: Bank Stress Index Volatility Fear Crush Reversal

data.source: Price_only adapter for financial sector ETF XLF and VIX via Yahoo Finance; daily data

behavior: VIX spikes >25 while XLF underperforms SPY by >2%, then reverses sharply as Fed rate-cut expectations emerge, driving financials outperformance.

market_impact.rationale: Financial sector rallies when fear subsides and rate expectations stabilize in their favor.

trade_logic.entry: If VIX > 25 and XLF lags SPY by >2% over 3 days, then VIX falls >5 points in 1 day, long XLF
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if XLF underperforms again

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Daily VIX and ETF data; fear/relief cycles occur 4–6 times per quarter in response to Fed announcements and earnings surprises.
